Notice Of Motion Seeking Leave To File First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Jan-09-2017 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES - ATTY. GEORGE R. NEMIROFF OF THE LAW OFFICE OF KENNETH C. ABSALOM, TEL. NO. 1-415-392-5040 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. AMY D. SUPER, DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY, TEL. NO. 1-415-554-3931 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Plaintiff Greg Blatman's motion for leave to file a first amended complaint is denied without prejudice to being refiled if the trial date is continued. Assuming without deciding that the proposed new claims are procedurally and substantively valid, adding new claims at this late stage of the case (eight days before trial) that are based in whole or in part on a theory of constructive termination in 2016 is unduly prejudicial to defendants. If the motion were granted, defendants would be required to proceed to trial on the new claims without any opportunity to take discovery. The new claims are not so related to the existing claims that it can be fairly said that defendants have no need for any discovery on those claims. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR # 8189 =302/hk
